commit.message:
Enable TUnit test report artifact upload in GitHub Actions (#902)

TUnit can automatically upload HTML test reports as workflow artifacts
and link them in the step summary when running in GitHub Actions — but
only if `ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN` is available to the shell process, which
it isn't by default (it's only exposed inside JS/Docker action
handlers).

## Changes

- Added an **"Expose GitHub Actions Runtime"** step using
`actions/github-script@v7` immediately before the `dotnet test` step in
both workflow files:
  - `.github/workflows/PR-Build-And-Test.yml`
  - `.github/workflows/Build-Test-And-Deploy.yml`

```yaml
- name: Expose GitHub Actions Runtime
  uses: actions/github-script@v7
  with:
    script: |
      core.exportVariable('ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN', process.env['ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN']);
      core.exportVariable('ACTIONS_RESULTS_URL', process.env['ACTIONS_RESULTS_URL']);

- name: Run .NET Tests
  run: dotnet test ...
```

With this in place, TUnit will upload the test report as a workflow
artifact and add a direct link to it in the step summary on each run.

commit.message:
Fix feedback Turnstile execute flow on glossboss.pages.dev (#24)

Feedback submission was consistently failing on `glossboss.pages.dev`
with Turnstile’s unauthorized-hostname error. The actual issue was in
the client-side Turnstile integration: the feedback flow was resetting
the widget immediately before every `execute()`, even though the widget
had already been rendered and was otherwise ready.

- **Turnstile execute flow**
- Update `src/lib/feedback/turnstile.ts` to execute the rendered widget
directly
- Remove the unconditional `reset(widgetId)` that ran immediately before
`execute(widgetId)`
- Keep the existing reset behavior for recoverable widget errors and
expired challenges

- **Regression coverage**
- Update `src/lib/feedback/turnstile.test.ts` so the first execute path
no longer expects a pre-execute reset
- Add a focused retry regression test to verify that retries still work
after a recoverable Turnstile error

- **Resulting behavior**
- The feedback modal continues to initialize the Turnstile widget
normally
- Submitting feedback now executes the existing widget instance instead
of resetting it first
- Recoverable Turnstile errors still reset the widget so the next
attempt starts cleanly

## Testing

- `bun run test -- src/lib/feedback/turnstile.test.ts
src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx`
- `bun run lint`
- `bun run format:check`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`
- `bun run test:coverage`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Pre-compute solvable append in background worker

When the board detects no moves left, highlightAppend() now
immediately starts a dedicated append worker computing the best
solvable arrangement. By the time the user reads the hint and
clicks Append, the result is typically ready (0ms).

Architecture:
- Separate appendWorker (from same blob as genWorker) avoids
  queue contention with board generation
- Worker uses quickTry(): 30 greedy attempts per candidate
  (lighter than full tryBoard's 100) with 8-second time budget
- Tries standard order first, then up to 500 shuffled arrangements
- doAppend() is now async: uses pre-computed result if ready,
  shows 'Calculating...' and awaits worker if not
- computeAppendSync() retained as fallback for edge cases

Performance (worst case — instant click, no reaction time):
- Hard board (14 remaining): 284ms
- Expert board (14 remaining): 149ms
- With normal user reaction (~500ms): 0ms (worker already done)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address multi-model code review: 7 API design fixes

1. Merge ProcessingGroup into ActorRuntime (GAT constraint fix)
   - Eliminates u8 witness hack that prevented generic group usage
   - join_group/leave_group/broadcast_group/get_group_members now on ActorRuntime

2. Remove RequestReserialization from VersionMismatchPolicy
   - Unimplementable: no version-aware serialization methods exist

3. Remove request<R> from ActorRef (type safety fix)
   - Allowed arbitrary reply types unrelated to message type
   - dstate uses fire-and-forget messaging; request-reply is adapter-specific
   - Also removes ActorRequestError type

4. StatePersistence now operates on StateObject<S>
   - Preserves metadata (age, incarnation, version, timestamps) through restart

5. Add pending_remote_incarnation to StateViewObject
   - Fixes incarnation-based staleness detection across owner restarts

6. ClusterEvents::subscribe returns SubscriptionId + add unsubscribe()
   - Prevents memory leaks from unremovable callbacks

7. TestActorRef::request removal (resolved by #3)

Tests: 50 -> 51 (removed 2 request tests, added 3 new tests)
All tests pass, zero clippy warnings.

commit.message:
feat: add maui-sherpa CLI with AI-agent discoverability (#91)

* feat: add maui-sherpa CLI with AI-agent discoverability

Add a new dotnet tool (MauiSherpa.Cli) that exposes MAUI Sherpa's
capabilities as a command-line interface designed for discoverability
by Copilot CLI and other AI code agents.

Command tree:
- features       — JSON manifest of all capabilities (AI discovery)
- doctor         — environment health check (.NET SDK, Android SDK, JDK, Xcode)
- android sdk    — SDK info, packages, install/uninstall
- android emulators — AVD list, start, stop
- android devices — connected device listing
- android keystores — create keystores, view signatures
- apple simulators — list, boot, shutdown, create
- apple devices  — physical iOS device listing
- apple xcode    — Xcode installation info
- workloads      — .NET workload list, sets, detailed info

Key design:
- --json global flag on every command for machine-readable output
- Rich --help descriptions for AI agent tool discovery
- Lightweight: depends only on System.CommandLine + MauiSherpa.Workloads
- Wraps underlying tools (adb, xcrun, keytool, sdkmanager, xcodebuild)
- Packaged as dotnet tool (PackAsTool) for global/local install

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(cli): add provisioning profile management commands

Add 'apple profiles' subcommand with list, show, install, and remove.
Decodes .mobileprovision files via 'security cms' to extract name, UUID,
team, type (Development/Ad Hoc/App Store/Enterprise), entitlements,
bundle ID, expiration, and provisioned device count.

Profiles can be resolved by UUID, file path, or name substring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(cli): add --agent flag for AI agent remediation mode

When an AI agent (Copilot CLI, etc.) runs 'maui-sherpa doctor --agent',
issues produce structured JSON with:
- diagnostics: full check results
- prompt: the same remediation prompt the GUI would send to its inner Copilot session
- guidance: per-category step-by-step fix instructions
- suggestedCommands: concrete maui-sherpa commands to run
- references: documentation URLs

This lets the outer agent act on the fix prompts directly, instead of
starting a nested Copilot session.

The Remediation helper also supports BuildProcessFailure and
BuildOperationFailure for future use by other commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(cli): make --agent flag prominent in features manifest and help

An AI agent's first contact is either --help or 'features'. Both now

* feat(cli): add provisioning profile creation via App Store Connect API

Add 'create' and 'asc-list' subcommands to 'maui-sherpa apple profiles':

commit.message:
feat: simplify LED to 3 field-essential patterns

Replace 8 LED states with 4 (OFF, BOOTING, BUSY, DONE, ERROR):
- BUSY: single fast blink for syncing/verifying/erasing (don't unplug)
- DONE: rapid burst + 3s solid (safe to unplug) — used for both
  success and already-empty
- ERROR: SOS repeating (any error)
- BOOTING: slow heartbeat for boot readiness (used by boot service)

A pilot in the field only needs to know three things: working, done,
or problem. The web UI /status endpoint still reports detailed
sub-states (syncing, verifying, erasing) for those who want more info.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: Reject blocks produced before their scheduled time

Production side: produce_block_at_height refuses to create a block
before genesis_timestamp + (height * BLOCK_TIME_SECONDS).

Validation side: validate_block rejects recent incoming blocks whose
timestamp is before their scheduled time (30s grace for clock skew).
Historical blocks during sync are exempt.

This prevents a malicious node from producing blocks early to claim
a higher chain and stall other nodes.

commit.message:
Charts: Fix composition legend position and alignment (#47478)

* fix(charts): Respect legend position in composition API

Composition legends (<Chart.Legend position="top" />) always appeared
at the bottom because they were rendered in the children slot, which
comes after the chart in the Stack layout. Alignment and orientation
worked since they affect Legend styling; position controls layout
placement and was ignored.

Extend useChartChildren to extract Legend children, read their position
prop, and return legendChildren. Charts now render legendChildren in
the top or bottom slot based on position, so composition legends honor
position="top" and position="bottom" across LineChart, BarChart,
PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart, and LeaderboardChart.

Fixes CHARTS-91.

Made-with: Cursor

* Fix args on LineChart story

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(charts): Remove unreleased changelog entry

Manual edits to the changelog are not the way.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Reuse LegendProps position type in useChartChildren

LegendChild duplicated the Legend component's position type ('top' | 'bottom'). Using NonNullable<LegendProps['position']> keeps the type in sync with the Legend component and avoids drift when position options change (e.g. future 'left' | 'right' support noted in Legend types).

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove obsolete useHasLegendChild hook

useChartChildren now extracts Legend components with position support
for alignment options. Charts derive "has legend" from
legendChildren.length instead of a separate hook call, so
useHasLegendChild became redundant. Removing it shrinks the public
API and keeps legend detection in one place.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add changelog entries.

* refactor(charts): Extract renderLegendSlot and add LegendPosition type

Each chart duplicated the same filter/map logic to render legend children by position. renderLegendSlot centralizes this.

LegendPosition provides a single source of truth for 'top' | 'bottom'
across the legend component, chart props, and composition hooks,
avoiding drift when adding 'left' | 'right' support later.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Remove duplicate Legend extraction tests from useChartChildren

Tests at lines 67–88 duplicated tests at 159–177 (default position /
position top). Collapse into the more comprehensive set at 159–191,
which also covers multiple legends.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(charts): Correct PR 47478 changelog scope and significance

The github-actions bot incorrectly added changelog entries to all
plugins that depend on charts. Only the charts package was modified;
plugins merely consume the fix. Removed the eight incorrect plugin
entries (backup, boost, jetpack, protect, search, social,
starter-plugin, videopress).

Updated the charts entry to significance: minor and type: changed.
The Legend position fix is a patch-level bugfix, but removing
useHasLegendChild from @automattic/charts/hooks is a breaking API
change. Pre-1.0, we treat breaking changes as minor bumps.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant LegendPosition cast in useChartChildren

The position value (child.props?.position ?? 'bottom') already satisfies
LegendPosition. The explicit cast was unnecessary since TypeScript infers
the type from the default. Minor formatting tweaks.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Wire legend props to Storybook args in composition story

WithCompositionLegend lacked control over orientation, alignment, and position,
so users couldn't interactively explore composition legend layouts. Binding
these props to story args makes the story more demonstrative.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Simplify legend alignment SCSS structure

Alignment was duplicated under each position (top/bottom), and vertical vs
horizontal legends use different axes (align-items vs justify-content).
Flattening the structure: alignment at root level, vertical overrides use
align-items, horizontal uses justify-content. Add align-self: stretch so
legend spans container width.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove unused legend position styles

The position-top and position-bottom classes only set position: relative
with no layout effect. Drop the classes and their styles to reduce
unused CSS. Keep position prop for composition API slot placement.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Clarify legend position prop and fix bar chart example

Position controls slot placement in the composition API, not visual styling.
Update JSDoc and API docs to reflect this. Fix BarChart docs example:
legendOrientation is not a valid prop; use orientation instead.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Add unit tests for renderLegendSlot

Covers empty input, position filtering, key generation, and ordering.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Remove unused position prop from BaseLegend

Position is read by chart composition from child.props for slot placement;
BaseLegend never used it. Dropping the param and eslint-disable.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Simplify LeaderboardChart empty state layout

Remove legend slots from the empty state — legends add noise when there is
no data. Drop redundant composition API comments.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use explicit bottom check for PieSemiCircle legend

legendPosition !== 'top' was equivalent but obscured intent. Match
LeaderboardChart pattern for consistency.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Default invalid legend position to bottom

Only 'top' and 'bottom' are valid. Invalid values (e.g. 'left' passed
before implementation) now fall back to 'bottom' instead of rendering
in wrong slots.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Rename otherChildren to nonLegendChildren

Clearer name for children excluding Legend. Add nonLegendChildren
returning all non-Legend children in original order (otherChildren
categorized by compound type). Update BarChart, LineChart, LeaderboardChart.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Add composition legend tests for all chart types

Verify composition Legend renders as child, ignores showLegend prop,
and respects position (top vs bottom) across BarChart, LineChart,
PieChart, and PieSemiCircleChart.

Made-with: Cursor

commit.message:
Setting V2 experience as default (#343)

This pull request introduces several enhancements and improvements to
the Azure AI resources utility (`_resources.py`) and updates the Cosmos
DB API documentation in the Azure AI agents demo notebook. The most
significant changes are the addition of environment variable support for
endpoint configuration, improved endpoint validation and warnings, and a
more comprehensive and user-friendly answer for Cosmos DB APIs in the
demo notebook.

**Enhancements to Azure AI resource configuration and validation:**

* Added support for environment variables (`AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT`,
`AZURE_AI_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`, `AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT`,
`AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION`, `AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME`) to
automatically resolve endpoint, project endpoint, model, and API version
when not explicitly provided in constructor parameters. This allows for
more flexible and convenient configuration in different environments.
* Implemented endpoint URL validation and warning logic via the new
`_validate_endpoint_url` function, which helps users avoid common
misconfigurations (e.g., using the wrong endpoint type, missing version
segments, or using non-HTTPS URLs in production).
* Added async token provider support with `_make_async_token_provider`,
enabling proper integration with asynchronous OpenAI clients.

**Improvements to Cosmos DB API documentation in the demo notebook:**

* Updated the Cosmos DB API answer to provide a clearer, more
structured, and comprehensive overview of the available APIs, including
a breakdown by API type, usage notes, and direct links to relevant
documentation. The answer now better guides users to the right resources
and clarifies how to select and use each API.
* Minor updates to execution counts and cell outputs in the notebook to
reflect improved error/warning handling and more accurate demonstration
of the agent's behavior.

commit.message:
LRU cache of 5 conversation scroll views for position retention

Keep up to 5 conversation ScrollViews alive in a ZStack so SwiftUI
naturally preserves scroll position when switching between them.

Chat model changes:
- messages is now a computed property backed by cachedMessages dict
- cachedConversationIds tracks LRU order, evicts oldest beyond 5
- loadConversation skips API call if conversation is already cached
- messages(for:) provides per-conversation access for views

View changes (all platforms):
- chatMessages renders a ZStack of cached conversation ScrollViews
- Only the active conversation is visible and interactive
- Each ScrollView has stable identity via conversation ID

commit.message:
feat(p10): Mixed reality passthrough — H.U.G.H. traverses digital and physical

Quest 3 AR/MR capabilities:
- immersive-ar session with camera-access + hit-test + anchors + depth-sensing
- HitTestReticle: surface detection via useXRHitTest (v6 API), entity snap-to-plane in AR mode
- Dual mode HUD: Enter VR (opaque) + Enter MR (passthrough) buttons
- useHughVision hook: 30s auto-capture + on-demand vision analysis
- XRModeDetector: AR mode disables skybox, enables passthrough rendering
- /api/vision proxy → LFM 2.5 inference for spatial reasoning (vite.config.ts)
- Single unified xrStore: enterVR() / enterAR() on same store avoids nested XR conflict

Convex schema additions:
- workshop_entities: +anchorId, +arPlaneId, +realWorldPosition
- ar_observations table: H.U.G.H. vision log with spatial analysis
- logARObservation mutation + getRecentObservations query

Note: useXRHitTest (not useHitTest) is the correct v6.6.29 API.
useHitTest was a v5/@react-three/drei name — v6 renamed it useXRHitTest
with signature (fn, relativeTo, trackableType).

H.U.G.H. now sees what the operator sees and can reason about the
physical lab space in real-time.
